justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO-monitoring gap — called out as 'CRITICAL: No renewal_scheduling_lag metric' and listed as P0 #1. (2) Retry tracking gap identified — 'CRITICAL: No retry_attempts_total metric' with explicit link to 24-hour retry SLO. (3) Current alert critiqued as symptom-based — 'The current error-count alert is a proxy that misses silent failures entirely.' (4) Specific metric recommendations: renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram, renewal_retry_attempts_total counter, renewal_retry_pending gauge, queue depth/age gauges. (5) SLO-based alerting recommended — P0 #2 recommends alerting on '% of renewals with lag > 3600s crosses 0.1% threshold', and P2 #13 mentions burn-rate alerting. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — goes well beyond rubric requirements with queue depth gaps, silent scheduler death scenario, circuit breaker recommendations, and health check critique
Clearly articulates why the current alert is insufficient: 'may fire when renewals are still succeeding or fail to fire when renewals are silently not being scheduled'
Priority-ranked recommendations (P0/P1/P2) with clear rationale for each
Strong framing: 'This service has a business SLO it cannot measure' — cuts directly to the core issue
Identifies failure modes beyond the rubric scope: silent scheduler death, queue backup, slow API degradation
Escalations are well-routed with actionable next steps
